title: भैरव चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
श्री भैरव चरणन सरोज, मन में धरि ध्यान।
चालीसा कहूँ भक्ति से, करो कृपा भगवान॥
काल भैरव शिवावतारी, रक्षक तुम संसार।
करो कृपा दासन पर, हरो सकल भय भार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय बटुक भैरव भगवाना।
तुम शिव के अंश सुजाना॥
काल भैरव विश्वनाथ के वारे।
काशी के कोतवाल हमारे॥
श्वान वाहन तुम्हरे सोहे।
भक्तन के मन नित प्रभु मोहे॥
त्रिशूल डमरू खप्पर धारी।
नागन से शोभित तन भारी॥
रुण्ड मुण्ड की माला पहने।
दिगम्बर रूप अतुल बल सहने॥
ब्रह्मा का पंचम शिर काटा।
कपाल में ब्रह्महत्या का पाटा॥
काशी में शाप से मुक्त भये।
कपाल मोचन तीर्थ बने नये॥
अष्ट भैरव रूप तिहारे।
असितांग चण्ड रुरु प्यारे॥
क्रोध उन्मत्त कपाली भीमा।
संहार भैरव अति उत्तमा॥
बावन भैरव मंदिर जाना।
सब शक्तिपीठ में तुम्हरा स्थाना॥
रात्रि समय विचरण करते।
भूत प्रेत पिशाच निवारते॥
काल भैरव अष्टमी पूजन।
भक्त जनन का कष्ट विमोचन॥
श्मशान भूमि निवास तिहारा।
तांत्रिक साधन का अधिकारा॥
क्षेत्रपाल रूप में रक्षक।
मंदिर ग्राम नगर के भक्षक॥
चोर डाकू भय से काँपें।
भैरव नाम सुने तन थाँपें॥
न्याय करो तुम सबका स्वामी।
पाप शमन करो अंतर्यामी॥
सरसों तेल दिया जो जलावे।
भैरव बाबा प्रसन्न हो जावे॥
शनिवार को पूजा करिये।
काला वस्त्र भैरव को धरिये॥
मदिरा नैवेद्य जो अर्पे।
भैरव बाबा शीघ्र प्रसन्ने॥
राहु केतु शनि ग्रह शान्ति।
भैरव पूजन से मिले विरान्ति॥
सर्प दोष कालसर्प योगा।
भैरव कृपा से मिटे वियोगा॥
मंगल दोष निवारण होवे।
भैरव पूजन सब दोष खोवे॥
जो नित भैरव ध्यान लगावे।
सो सब भय संकट से बचावे॥
शत्रु नाश करो भगवाना।
भक्त जनन को दो कल्याणा॥
चालीसा भैरव का गावे।
सो सब सुख सम्पत्ति पावे॥
रोग शोक भय दूर करावे।
भैरव चरण शरण जो आवे॥
प्रेत बाधा सब दूर भगावे।
काल भैरव जो नाम सुनावे॥
जन्म मरण के भय हरिये।
भैरव कृपा से मुक्ति करिये॥
चालीसा पढ़ भक्ति बढ़ावे।
भैरव दासा सब सुख पावे॥

॥ दोहा ॥
भैरव चालीसा पढ़े, करे अष्टमी को पूज।
सकल विपत विनाश हो, मिले शिव कृपा अनूज॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Shri Bhairav Charanan Saroj, Man Mein Dhari Dhyan.
Chalisa Kahun Bhakti Se, Karo Kripa Bhagwan.
Kal Bhairav Shivavatari, Rakshak Tum Sansar.
Karo Kripa Dasan Par, Haro Sakal Bhay Bhar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Batuk Bhairav Bhagwana.
Tum Shiv Ke Ansh Sujana.
Kal Bhairav Vishwanath Ke Vare.
Kashi Ke Kotwal Hamare.
Shwan Vahan Tumhre Sohe.
Bhaktan Ke Man Nit Prabhu Mohe.
Trishul Damru Khappar Dhari.
Nagan Se Shobhit Tan Bhari.
Rund Mund Ki Mala Pahne.
Digambar Roop Atul Bal Sahne.
Brahma Ka Pancham Shir Kata.
Kapal Mein Brahmahatya Ka Pata.
Kashi Mein Shap Se Mukt Bhaye.
Kapal Mochan Tirth Bane Naye.
Ashta Bhairav Roop Tihare.
Asitang Chand Ruru Pyare.
Krodh Unmatt Kapali Bhima.
Sanhar Bhairav Ati Uttama.
Bavan Bhairav Mandir Jana.
Sab Shaktipeeth Mein Tumhra Sthana.
Ratri Samay Vicharan Karte.
Bhoot Pret Pishach Nivarte.
Kal Bhairav Ashtami Pujan.
Bhakt Janan Ka Kasht Vimochan.
Shamshan Bhumi Nivas Tihara.
Tantrik Sadhan Ka Adhikara.
Kshetrapal Roop Mein Rakshak.
Mandir Gram Nagar Ke Bhakshak.
Chor Daku Bhay Se Kampe.
Bhairav Naam Sune Tan Thampe.
Nyay Karo Tum Sabka Swami.
Paap Shaman Karo Antaryami.
Sarson Tel Diya Jo Jalave.
Bhairav Baba Prasann Ho Jave.
Shanivar Ko Puja Kariye.
Kala Vastra Bhairav Ko Dhariye.
Madira Naivedya Jo Arpe.
Bhairav Baba Shighra Prasanne.
Rahu Ketu Shani Grah Shanti.
Bhairav Pujan Se Mile Viranti.
Sarp Dosh Kalsarp Yoga.
Bhairav Kripa Se Mite Viyoga.
Mangal Dosh Nivaran Hove.
Bhairav Pujan Sab Dosh Khove.
Jo Nit Bhairav Dhyan Lagave.
So Sab Bhay Sankat Se Bachave.
Shatru Nash Karo Bhagwana.
Bhakt Janan Ko Do Kalyana.
Chalisa Bhairav Ka Gave.
So Sab Sukh Sampatti Pave.
Rog Shok Bhay Door Karave.
Bhairav Charan Sharan Jo Ave.
Pret Badha Sab Door Bhagave.
Kal Bhairav Jo Naam Sunave.
Janm Maran Ke Bhay Hariye.
Bhairav Kripa Se Mukti Kariye.
Chalisa Padh Bhakti Badhave.
Bhairav Dasa Sab Sukh Pave.

|| Doha ||
Bhairav Chalisa Padhe, Kare Ashtami Ko Pooj.
Sakal Vipat Vinash Ho, Mile Shiv Kripa Anooj.
hindiMeaning:
श्री भैरव के चरण कमलों में ध्यान धरकर भक्ति से चालीसा कहता हूँ। काल भैरव शिव के अवतार हैं, संसार के रक्षक हैं। दासों पर कृपा करो और सकल भय का भार हरो।

जय बटुक भैरव भगवान! आप शिव के अंश हैं। काल भैरव विश्वनाथ के द्वारपाल, काशी के कोतवाल हैं। श्वान आपका वाहन है।
benefits: Powerful protection against evil spirits, black magic, and negative energies
Traditionally recited for removal of kalsarpa dosha and malefic planetary effects of rahu-ketu-shani
Invokes fearlessness and victory over enemies
Guards homes, temples, and villages from theft and crime
Essential prayer for Kaal Bhairav Ashtami worship
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Bhairav Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as भैरव चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Bhairava. This sacred composition is sourced from Traditional Chalisa Sangrah and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to guard homes, temples, and villages from theft and crime and grant fearlessness and victory over enemies. It is also said to remove Kalsarpa Dosha and malefic planetary effects of Rahu-Ketu-Shani. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kaal Bhairav Ashtami for enhanced blessings. With 42 verses and a reading time of about 15 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Bhairav Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Bhairav Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Powerful prayer to Kaal Bhairav for protection from evil and planetary afflictions.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Bhairava?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhairava is the fierce manifestation of Lord Shiva, embodying the destructive aspect of time, dharmic justice and the dissolution of ego. The Pauranika narrative in the Shiva Purana describes how Bhairava emerged from Shiva's third eye to humble Brahma, who had grown arrogant and claimed equality with the Supreme. Bhairava severed Brahma's fifth head, after which he had to wander the earth as Kapali (skull-bearer) until he reached Kashi (Varanasi), where the brahmahatya curse was finally lifted. He is therefore the supreme protector of Kashi and bears the title Kshetrapala — guardian of every sacred precinct. The Kashi Kaal Bhairav temple is his most renowned shrine. (Sources: Shiva Purana; Wikipedia Bhairava; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Bhairav Chalisa and what is its structure?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhairav Chalisa is a 40-verse Hindi devotional composition in chaupai metre, with framing dohas at the beginning and end bringing the total recitation to about 43 verse-units. The word chalisa derives from chalis meaning forty. The text praises Kaal Bhairav as the supreme guardian of devotees, narrates his iconography (black complexion, fierce eyes, trident, damaru, sword, dog vahana, garland of skulls), recounts his role as Kshetrapala of Kashi, and lists his eight principal forms (Ashta Bhairava). The closing dohas form the phalashruti, promising freedom from fear, protection from enemies and supernatural disturbances, and removal of Shani and Rahu doshas. (Sources: Hindupedia Bhairava; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Bhairav Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) are the traditional weekdays of Bhairava worship; Sunday is also recommended in some lineages. The supreme annual occasion is Kaal Bhairav Ashtami, falling on Margashirsha Krishna Ashtami (November-December), the day Bhairava is said to have manifested. Other peak occasions include every Ashtami of the dark fortnight (Krishna Paksha Ashtami), Mahashivaratri, the eight days following Holi, and any Saturday during Sade Sati or Dhaiya periods when devotees seek Bhairava's protection from Shani's effects. The traditional timing is at midnight (nishita kaal) for tantric practitioners and at sunset for household devotees. (Sources: Drikpanchang Kaal Bhairav Ashtami; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Bhairav Chalisa confer?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti tradition associated with the Chalisa promises: complete protection from enemies, ghosts, pretas and supernatural disturbances; removal of Shani Sade Sati and Rahu-Ketu doshas (Bhairava is considered the controller of these grahas); fearlessness in legal matters, court cases and conflicts; freedom from addictions and harmful habits through disciplined nightly recitation; recovery of lost wealth and property; protection during travel especially at night; healing of chronic illnesses associated with the lower body; and ultimately the grace of Mahakaal Shiva himself. Bhairava is considered particularly responsive to devotees in genuine distress and is the Kotwal (police-chief) of Kashi who responds quickly to invocations. (Sources: Hindupedia; Drikpanchang; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Kaal Bhairav and Batuk Bhairav?
seoFaqs.answer: Kaal Bhairav is the elder, fierce, full-grown form of Bhairava — black-complexioned, naked, garlanded with skulls, accompanied by the dog vahana, and presiding over Kashi as Kshetrapala. Batuk Bhairav is the youthful boy form (batuk meaning young boy), typically depicted as a five-year-old child with a peaceful smile, holding a trident or staff. Batuk Bhairav is considered more accessible for household worship and is invoked particularly for the protection of children, blessings for childless couples, and gentler protective grace. The Batuk Bhairav Stotra and the Batuk Bhairav Sahasranama are popular tantric texts in his worship. Most temples worship both forms; the choice depends on the specific intent of the devotee. (Sources: Wikipedia Bhairava; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Are there any special restrictions on Bhairav Chalisa recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chalisa itself, being a Hindi devotional composition rather than a tantric mantra, is open to all devotees without initiation. The traditional preparations include cleanliness, an east or south-facing seat, lighting a sesame oil or mustard oil lamp (Bhairava's preferred oils), offering of black sesame, urad dal, jalebi or sweet bread, and ideally a small offering of food to a stray dog after the recitation (since the dog is Bhairava's vahana). Tantric Bhairava worship involving advanced kriyas, cremation-ground rituals or specific bija-mantra invocations does require guru initiation. For ordinary household recitation of the Chalisa, devotion and consistency matter more than ritual elaboration. (Sources: Hindupedia Bhairava; Sanatan Sanstha; Drikpanchang.)
